signals.headline: Tether Backs Drift Relaunch on Solana | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Tether Backs Drift Relaunch on Solana | YES | HIGH confidence**
Tether announced a $150 million recovery plan for users following a $285 million Drift exploit, expanding USDT's role on Solana[3]. This signals institutional confidence in Solana's ecosystem recovery.

signals.headline: Ethereum Posts 8
signals.raw:
Ethereum Posts 8.6% Daily Gain | YES | HIGH confidence**
ETH at $2,377 surged 8.6% on the day, pushing price above $2,350 resistance[2]. A close above $2,500 would confirm a breakout and likely drive rotation from ETH shorts into spot accumulation[2].

signals.headline: Solana Hits Record 167M Token Holders | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Hits Record 167M Token Holders | YES | HIGH confidence**
Solana reached an all-time record of 167 million monthly token holders with daily non-vote transactions hitting 148 million[4], though capital inflows have not yet been strong enough to sustain price momentum[5].

signals.headline: Solana ETF Inflows Continue | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana ETF Inflows Continue | YES | HIGH confidence**
U.S. spot Solana ETFs recorded $5.04 million in net inflows on April 16, with cumulative net assets for SOL ETFs reaching $835 million[3]. This signals sustained institutional demand and provides underlying price support.

signals.headline: Solana Reclaims $85 Level | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Reclaims $85 Level | YES | HIGH confidence**
SOL climbed 6.3% on the day to $86.55, holding above its 200-day moving average and reclaiming the $85 resistance level[2]. A weekly close above $92 opens room toward $105[2].
